requirements.full_sentence:
भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
RBI/DOR/2025-26/300
DOR.CAP.REC.219/09-18-201/2025-26

 November 28, 2025
Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Prudential Norms on
Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025 (Updated as on May 15, 2026)
Table of Contents
requirements.object: Contents

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect immediately upon issuance.
requirements.object: upon issuance

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Rural Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'RCBs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'RCB').
requirements.object: Co-operative Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'RCBs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'RCB')

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of these Directions, Rural Co-operative Banks shall mean State
Co-operative Banks and Central Co-operative Banks, as defined in the National
Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development Act, 1981.
requirements.object: and Central Co-operative Banks, as defined in the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development Act, 1981

requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context states otherwise, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Credit Risk' is defined as the potential that a bank's borrower or counterparty
may fail to meet its obligations in accordance with agreed terms.
requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Credit Risk' is defined as the potential that a bank's borrower or counterparty may fail to meet its ob

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Deferred Tax Assets' and 'Deferred Tax Liabilities' shall have the same meaning
as assigned under the applicable Accounting Standards;
requirements.object: as assigned under the applicable Accounting Standards

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Derivative' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in Section 45U(a) of
the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934;
requirements.object: as assigned to it in Section 45U(a) of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(4) 'Other Approved Securities' shall have the same meaning as defined under
Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and
Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025;
requirements.object: as defined under Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions,

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) 'Public Financial Institution' shall have the same meaning as defined under sub-
section 2(72) of the Companies Act, 2013.
requirements.object: as defined under sub- section 2(72) of the Companies Act, 2013

requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the BR Act, 1949, or the RBI Act, 1934, or
respective State Co-operative Societies Act and rules / regulations made
thereunder, or any statutory modification or re-enactment thereto or as used in
commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the BR Act, 1949, or the RBI Act, 1934, or respective State Co-operative Societies Act and r

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of the provisions contained in Section 11 read with Section 56 of the BR
Act, 1949, no co-operative bank shall commence or carry-on banking business
unless the aggregate value of its paid-up capital and reserves is not less than
one lakh of rupees.
requirements.object: business unless the aggregate value of its paid-up capital and reserves is not less than one lakh of rupees

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of this Section, 'value' means the real or
exchangeable value and not the nominal value which may be shown in the books
of the co-operative bank concerned.
requirements.object: books of the co-operative bank concerned

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of the provisions contained in
Section 42(6)(a)(i) of the RBI Act, 1934, inter alia, a scheduled state co-operative
bank is required to maintain paid-up capital and reserves of an aggregate value
of not less than five lakh rupees.
requirements.object: and reserves of an aggregate value of not less than five lakh rupees

requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall maintain a minimum Capital to Risk Weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR)
of 9 per cent on an ongoing basis.
requirements.object: to Risk Weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) of 9 per cent on an ongoing basis

requirements.full_sentence:
The computation of CRAR shall be as under:
Eligible total capital
CRAR =
Total Risk-weighted Assets (RWAs)
requirements.object: total capital CRAR = Total Risk-weighted Assets (RWAs)

requirements.full_sentence:
The capital funds (eligible total capital) for capital adequacy purposes shall consist
of Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital as defined below.
requirements.object: and Tier 2 capital as defined below

requirements.full_sentence:
RWAs shall be calculated as per
Paragraph 17 given below.
requirements.object: Paragraph 17 given below

requirements.full_sentence:
Tier 1 Capital shall include the following:
<image>
Note: An RCB is permitted to raise share capital, as hitherto, by way of (a)
issue of shares to persons within its area of operation, in accordance with
the provisions of its bye-laws, and (b) issue of additional shares to the
existing members;
requirements.object: Note: An RCB is permitted to raise share capital, as hitherto, by way of (a) issue of shares to persons within its area

requirements.full_sentence:
Note -
(1) Instructions for treatment of Bad and Doubtful Debt Reserve (BDDR) for
prudential purposes:
Note - An RCB shall also refer to Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
Banks - Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025 in this regard.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025 in this regard

requirements.full_sentence:
PNCPS issued by an RCB shall adhere to the following guidelines for to be
eligible for inclusion in Tier 1 capital:
(1) An RCB is permitted to issue PNCPS at face value to its members or any other
person residing within its area of operation, with the prior approval of the Reserve
Bank.
requirements.object: guidelines for to be eligible for inclusion in Tier 1 capital: (1) An RCB is permitted to issue PNCPS at face value to its mem

requirements.full_sentence:
The RCB shall submit the application seeking permission, together with
the prospectus / offer document / information memorandum, through the Pravaah
Portal of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: permission, together with the prospectus / offer document / information memorandum, through the Pravaah Portal of the R

requirements.full_sentence:
A certificate from a Chartered Accountant to the
effect that the terms of the offer document are in compliance with these
<image>
instructions shall also be submitted along with the application.
requirements.object: with the application

requirements.full_sentence:
The amounts
raised through PNCPS shall comply with the following terms and conditions to
qualify for inclusion as Tier 1 capital.
requirements.object: terms and conditions to qualify for inclusion as Tier 1 capital

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Limits
The outstanding amount of PNCPS and PDI along with outstanding IPDI shall
not exceed 35 per cent of total Tier 1 Capital at any point of time.
requirements.object: of total Tier 1 Capital at any point of time

requirements.full_sentence:
The above limit
shall be based on the amount of Tier 1 Capital after deduction of goodwill and
other intangible assets, but before deduction of equity investment in subsidiaries,
if any.
requirements.object: amount of Tier 1 Capital after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets, but before deduction of equity investment in subsidiaries

requirements.full_sentence:
PNCPS issued in excess of the overall ceiling of 35 per cent, shall be
eligible for inclusion under upper Tier 2 Capital, subject to limits prescribed for
Tier 2 Capital.
requirements.object: under upper Tier 2 Capital

requirements.full_sentence:
However, investors' rights and obligations shall remain
unchanged.

(3) Amount
The amount of PNCPS to be raised shall be decided by the Board of Directors of
an RCB.
requirements.object: Board of Directors of an RCB

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Maturity
The PNCPS shall be perpetual.
(i) PNCPS shall not be issued with a 'put option' or 'step up option'.
requirements.object: 'put option' or 'step up option'

requirements.full_sentence: (ii) PNCPS may be issued with a 'call option', subject to following conditions:
requirements.object: 'call option'

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Call option shall be exercised only with the prior approval of the
Reserve Bank (Department of Regulation).
requirements.object: the prior approval of the Reserve Bank (Department of Regulation)

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Classification in the Balance Sheet
<image>
These instruments shall be classified as 'Capital' and shown separately in the
Balance Sheet.
requirements.object: and shown separately in the Balance Sheet

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Dividend
The rate of dividend payable to the investors shall be a fixed rate or a floating
rate referenced to a market determined rupee interest benchmark rate.
requirements.object: or a floating rate referenced to a market determined rupee interest benchmark rate

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The payment of dividend by an RCB shall be subject to availability of
distributable surplus out of current year's profits, and if:
requirements.object: of distributable surplus out of current year's profits, and if:

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The dividend shall not be cumulative, i.e., dividend missed in a year shall
not be paid in subsequent years even if adequate profit is available and the
level of CRAR conforms to the regulatory minimum.

conditions:
When dividend is paid
at a rate lesser than the prescribed rate
requirements.full_sentence:
When dividend is paid
at a rate lesser than the prescribed rate, the unpaid amount shall not be
paid in future years, even if adequate profit is available and the level of
CRAR conforms to the regulatory minimum.
requirements.object: years, even if adequate profit is available and the level of CRAR conforms to the regulatory minimum

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) All instances of non-payment of dividend / payment of dividend at a rate
less than that specified shall be reported by the issuing RCB to the
respective Regional Office of National Bank for Agriculture and Rural
Development (NABARD) and Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank
(latter at email: capdor@rbi.org.in).
requirements.object: issuing RCB to the respective Regional Office of National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) and Department of Reg

requirements.full_sentence:
(9) Seniority of Claim
The claims of the investors in PNCPS shall be senior to the claims of investors
in equity shares and subordinated to the claims of all other creditors and the
depositors.
requirements.object: claims of investors in equity shares and subordinated to the claims of all other creditors and the depositors

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(10) Voting Rights
The investors in PNCPS shall not be eligible for any voting rights.
requirements.object: voting rights

requirements.full_sentence:
(11) Discount
The PNCPS shall not be subjected to a progressive discount for capital adequacy
purposes.
requirements.object: progressive discount for capital adequacy purposes

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) PNCPS shall be fully paid-up, unsecured, and free of any restrictive
clauses.
requirements.object: and free of any restrictive clauses

conditions:
if any, stipulated
by other regulatory authorities in regard to issue of the PNCPS
requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) An RCB shall also comply with the terms and conditions, if any, stipulated
by other regulatory authorities in regard to issue of the PNCPS, provided
they are not in conflict with the terms and conditions specified in these
guidelines.
requirements.object: terms and conditions

requirements.full_sentence:
Any instance of conflict shall be brought to the notice of the
Reserve Bank (Department of Regulation) for seeking confirmation of the
eligibility of the instrument for inclusion in Tier 1 Capital.
requirements.object: notice of the Reserve Bank (Department of Regulation) for seeking confirmation of the eligibility of the instrument for inclusion in

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The total amount raised by an RCB by issue of PNCPS shall not be
reckoned as liability for calculation of Net Demand and Time Liabilities
(NDTL) for the purpose of reserve requirements and, as such, shall not
attract CRR / SLR requirements.
requirements.object: for calculation of Net Demand and Time Liabilities (NDTL) for the purpose of reserve requirements and, as such, shall not att

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) However, the amount collected from members / prospective investors and
held pending allotment of the PNCPS, shall be reckoned as liability for the
purpose of calculating the NDTL and shall, accordingly, attract reserve
requirements.
requirements.object: for the purpose of calculating the NDTL and shall, accordingly, attract reserve requirements

requirements.full_sentence:
Such amounts shall not be reckoned for calculation of capital
funds.
requirements.object: of capital funds

requirements.full_sentence:
(14) Reporting Requirements
An RCB issuing PNCPS shall submit a report to the concerned Regional Office
of Department of Supervision, NABARD and Department of Regulation, Reserve
Bank (latter at email: capdor@rbi.org.in) giving details of the capital raised,
including the terms and conditions of issue together with a copy of the prospectus
/ offer document, soon after the issue is completed.
requirements.object: the concerned Regional Office of Department of Supervision, NABARD and Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank (latter at email: cap

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(15) Investments in PNCPS and Advances for Purchase of PNCPS
An RCB shall not grant any loan or advance to any person for purchasing its own
PNCPS or the PNCPS of other banks.
requirements.object: advance to any person for purchasing its own PNCPS or the PNCPS of other banks

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the RCB shall not invest in
PNCPS of other banks and shall not grant advances against the security of the
PNCPS issued by them or other banks.
requirements.object: other banks and shall not grant advances against the security of the PNCPS issued by them or other banks

requirements.full_sentence:
However, a State Co-operative Bank
may invest in PNCPS issued by Central Co-operative Banks affiliated to them,
subject to the condition that the amount so invested shall be deducted from its
Tier 1 Capital.
requirements.object: Tier 1 Capital

requirements.full_sentence:
PDIs issued by an RCB shall adhere to the following guidelines for to be eligible
for inclusion in Tier 1 Capital:
(1) An RCB may issue PDI as bonds or debentures to its members or any other
person residing within its area of operation, with the prior approval of the Reserve
Bank.
requirements.object: guidelines for to be eligible for inclusion in Tier 1 Capital: (1) An RCB may issue PDI as bonds or debentures to its members

requirements.full_sentence:
The RCB shall submit the application seeking permission, together with
the prospectus / offer document / information memorandum through the Pravaah
Portal of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: permission, together with the prospectus / offer document / information memorandum through the Pravaah Portal of the Re

requirements.full_sentence:
A certificate from a Chartered Accountant to the
effect that the terms of the offer document are in compliance with these
instructions shall also be submitted along with the application.
requirements.object: with the application

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Limits
The amount of PDI reckoned for Tier 1 Capital shall not exceed 15 per cent of
total Tier 1 Capital.
requirements.object: of total Tier 1 Capital

requirements.full_sentence:
The outstanding IPDI shall also be covered in the
aforementioned ceiling of 15 per cent and reckoned for capital purposes.
requirements.object: the aforementioned ceiling of 15 per cent and reckoned for capital purposes

requirements.full_sentence:
The
eligible amount shall be computed with reference to the amount of Tier 1 Capital
as on March 31 of the previous year, after deduction of goodwill, and other
intangible assets, but before deduction of equity investment in subsidiaries, if
any.
requirements.object: to the amount of Tier 1 Capital as on March 31 of the previous year, after deduction of goodwill, and other intangible assets, but before deduction of equity investment in subsidiaries

requirements.full_sentence:
PDI in excess of the above limits shall be eligible for inclusion under Tier 2
Capital, subject to the limits prescribed for Tier 2 Capital.
requirements.object: under Tier 2 Capital

requirements.full_sentence:
However, investors'
rights and obligations shall remain unchanged.

Note - Reference is invited to paragraph 11 as per which the outstanding amount
of PNCPS and PDI along with outstanding IPDI shall not exceed 35 per cent of
total Tier 1 Capital at any point of time.
requirements.object: of total Tier 1 Capital at any point of time

requirements.full_sentence:
Tier 2 Capital shall include the following:
Note - Outstanding Long Term (Subordinated) Deposits (LTD) shall also be
eligible to be reckoned as Tier 2 Capital subject to the ceilings prescribed
in Paragraph 14 and Paragraph 16.
requirements.object: - Outstanding Long Term (Subordinated) Deposits (LTD) shall also be eligible to be reckoned as Tier 2 Capital subject to t

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) General provisions and loss reserves
These shall include such provisions of general nature appearing in the
books of an RCB which are not attributed to any identified potential loss or
a diminution in value of an asset or a known liability.
requirements.object: general nature appearing in the books of an RCB which are not attributed to any identified potential loss or a diminution i

requirements.full_sentence:
Adequate care shall
be taken to ensure that sufficient provisions have been made to meet all
known losses and foreseeable potential losses before considering any
amount of general provision as part of Tier 2 Capital as indicated above.
requirements.object: that sufficient provisions have been made to meet all known losses and foreseeable potential losses before considering any amount o

requirements.full_sentence:
Such provisions which are considered for inclusion in Tier 2
Capital shall be admitted up to 1.25 per cent of total RWAs.

(ii) Investment Fluctuation Reserve (IFR)
Balance in IFR shall be eligible for inclusion in Tier 2 Capital.
requirements.object: in Tier 2 Capital

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Tier 2 Capital instruments
An RCB may issue the following instruments to augment its Tier 2 Capital:
requirements.object: to augment its Tier 2 Capital:

requirements.full_sentence:
The total of Tier 2 Capital shall be limited to a maximum of 100 per cent of total
Tier 1 Capital for the purpose of compliance with the capital adequacy
framework.
requirements.object: maximum of 100 per cent of total Tier 1 Capital for the purpose of compliance with the capital adequacy framework

requirements.full_sentence:
PCPS / RNCPS / RCPS issued by RCBs shall adhere to following criteria to be
eligible for inclusion in Upper Tier 2 Capital.
requirements.object: to be eligible for inclusion in Upper Tier 2 Capital

requirements.full_sentence:
The RCB shall submit the application seeking
permission, together with the prospectus / offer document / information
<image>
memorandum through the Pravaah Portal of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: permission, together with the prospectus / offer document / information <image> memorandum through the Pravaah Portal o

requirements.full_sentence:
A certificate from
a Chartered Accountant to the effect that the terms of the offer document are in
compliance with these instructions shall also be submitted along with the
application.
requirements.object: with the application

requirements.full_sentence:
These three instruments, collectively referred to as Tier 2 preference
shares, shall comply with the following terms and conditions, to qualify for
inclusion as upper Tier 2 capital.
requirements.object: terms and conditions, to qualify for inclusion as upper Tier 2 capital

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Limits
The outstanding amount of these instruments along with other components of
Tier 2 capital shall not exceed 100 per cent of Tier 1 capital at any point of time.
requirements.object: of Tier 1 capital at any point of time

requirements.full_sentence:
The above limit shall be based on the amount of Tier 1 capital after deduction of
goodwill and other intangible assets, but before deduction of equity investment
in subsidiaries, if any.
requirements.object: amount of Tier 1 capital after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets, but before deduction of equity investment in subsidiaries

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Amount
The amount to be raised shall be decided by the Board of Directors of an RCB.
requirements.object: Board of Directors of an RCB

requirements.full_sentence: (i) These instruments shall not be issued with a 'put option' or 'step up option'.
requirements.object: 'put option' or 'step up option'

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) These instruments may be issued with a 'call option', subject to following
conditions:
requirements.object: 'call option'

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Call option shall be exercised only with the prior approval of the
Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: the prior approval of the Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(6) Classification in the Balance Sheet
These instruments shall be classified as 'Borrowings' and shown separately in
the Balance Sheet.
requirements.object: and shown separately in the Balance Sheet

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Coupon
The coupon payable to the investors shall be either at a fixed rate or at a floating
rate referenced to a market determined rupee interest benchmark rate.
requirements.object: fixed rate or at a floating rate referenced to a market determined rupee interest benchmark rate

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The coupon payable on these instruments shall be treated as interest and
accordingly debited to Profit and Loss Account.
requirements.object: and accordingly debited to Profit and Loss Account

requirements.full_sentence: (c) The RCB shall not have a net loss.

(ii) In the case of PCPS and RCPS, the unpaid / partly unpaid coupon shall be
treated as a liability.
requirements.object: liability

requirements.full_sentence:
The interest amount due and remaining unpaid may
be allowed to be paid in later years subject to an RCB complying with the
above requirements.
requirements.object: paid in later years subject to an RCB complying with the above requirements

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) In the case of RNCPS, deferred coupon shall not be paid in future years,
even if adequate profit is available and the level of CRAR conforms to the
regulatory minimum.
requirements.object: years, even if adequate profit is available and the level of CRAR conforms to the regulatory minimum

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) All instances of non-payment of coupon or payment of coupon at a rate
lesser than the specified rate shall be reported by the issuing RCB to the
<image>
concerned Regional Office of NABARD and Department of Regulation,
Reserve Bank (latter at email: capdor@rbi.org.in).
requirements.object: issuing RCB to the <image> concerned Regional Office of NABARD and Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank (latter at email: capdor@

requirements.full_sentence:
(9) Redemption / Repayment of Redeemable Tier 2 Preference Shares
RNCPS and RCPS shall not be redeemable at the initiative of the holder.
requirements.object: initiative of the holder

requirements.full_sentence:
Redemption of these instruments at maturity shall be made only with the prior
approval of the Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank subject, inter alia, to
the following conditions:
requirements.object: the prior approval of the Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank subject, inter alia, to the following conditions:

requirements.full_sentence:
(10) Seniority of Claim
The claims of the investors in these instruments shall be senior to the claims of
investors in instruments eligible for inclusion in Tier 1 Capital and subordinate to
the claims of all other creditors including those in lower Tier 2 Capital and the
depositors.
requirements.object: claims of investors in instruments eligible for inclusion in Tier 1 Capital and subordinate to the claims of all other creditors incl

requirements.full_sentence:
Amongst the investors of various instruments included in upper Tier
2 Capital, the claims shall rank pari passu with each other.
requirements.object: each other

requirements.full_sentence:
(11) Voting Rights
The investors in Tier 2 preference shares shall not be eligible for any voting
rights.
requirements.object: voting rights

requirements.full_sentence:
(12) Progressive discount for the purpose of computing CRAR
The redeemable preference shares (both cumulative and non-cumulative) shall
be subjected to progressive discount for capital adequacy purposes over the last
five years of their tenor, as under:
Remaining Maturity of Instruments Tenor Rate of Discount (%)
Less than one year 100
One year and more but less than two years 80
Two years and more but less than three years 60
Three years and more but less than four years 40
Four years and more but less than five years 20
<image>
(13) Other Conditions
requirements.object: discount for capital adequacy purposes over the last five years of their tenor, as under: Remaining Maturity of Instrument

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The Tier 2 preference shares shall be fully paid-up, unsecured, and free of
any restrictive clauses.
requirements.object: and free of any restrictive clauses

conditions:
if any, stipulated
by other regulatory authorities in regard to issue of the Tier 2 preference
shares
requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) An RCB shall also comply with the terms and conditions, if any, stipulated
by other regulatory authorities in regard to issue of the Tier 2 preference
shares, provided they are not in conflict with any terms and conditions
specified in these guidelines.
requirements.object: terms and conditions

requirements.full_sentence:
Any instance of conflict shall be brought to the
notice of the Reserve Bank (Department of Regulation) for seeking
confirmation of the eligibility of the instrument for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.
requirements.object: notice of the Reserve Bank (Department of Regulation) for seeking confirmation of the eligibility of the instrument for inclusion in

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The total amount raised by an RCB through the issue of these instruments
shall be reckoned as liability for the calculation of NDTL for the purpose of
reserve requirements and, as such, shall attract CRR / SLR requirements.
requirements.object: for the calculation of NDTL for the purpose of reserve requirements and, as such, shall attract CRR / SLR requirements

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The amount collected from members / prospective investors and held
pending allotment shall not be reckoned for calculation of capital funds until
the allotment process is over.
requirements.object: of capital funds until the allotment process is over

requirements.full_sentence:
(15) Reporting Requirements
An RCB issuing these instruments shall submit a report to the concerned
Regional Office of Department of Supervision, NABARD, and Department of
Regulation, Reserve Bank (latter at email: capdor@rbi.org.in) giving details of
the capital raised, including the terms and conditions of issue together with a
copy of the prospectus / offer document soon after the issue is completed.
requirements.object: the concerned Regional Office of Department of Supervision, NABARD, and Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank (latter at email: ca

requirements.full_sentence:
(16) Investments in Tier 2 preference shares and advances for purchase of Tier 2
preference shares
An RCB shall not grant any loan or advance to any person for purchasing its own
Tier 2 preference shares or Tier 2 preference shares of other banks.
requirements.object: advance to any person for purchasing its own Tier 2 preference shares or Tier 2 preference shares of other banks

requirements.full_sentence:
The RCB
shall not invest in Tier 2 preference shares issued by other banks and shall not
grant advances against the security of Tier 2 preference shares issued by them
or other banks.
requirements.object: preference shares issued by other banks and shall not grant advances against the security of Tier 2 preference shares issued by them

requirements.full_sentence:
However, a State Co-operative Bank may invest in Tier 2
preference shares issued by a Central Co-operative Bank affiliated to them
<image>
subject to the condition that the amount so invested shall be deducted from Tier
2 Capital of the State Co-operative Bank.
requirements.object: 2 Capital of the State Co-operative Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The amounts raised through LTSB shall comply with
the following terms and conditions to be eligible for inclusion in Lower Tier 2
capital.
requirements.object: terms and conditions to be eligible for inclusion in Lower Tier 2 capital

requirements.full_sentence:
The RCB shall submit the application
seeking permission, together with the prospectus / offer document /
information memorandum through the Pravaah Portal of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: permission, together with the prospectus / offer document / information memorandum through the Pravaah Portal of the Re

requirements.full_sentence:
A certificate from a Chartered Accountant to the effect that the terms of the
offer document are in compliance with these instructions shall also be
submitted along with the application.
requirements.object: with the application

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) Limit
The amount of LTSB eligible to be reckoned as Tier 2 Capital shall be limited to
50 per cent of total Tier 1 Capital.
requirements.object: per cent of total Tier 1 Capital

requirements.full_sentence:
The outstanding Long Term (Subordinated)
Deposits (LTDs shall also be covered in the aforementioned ceiling of 50 per
cent and reckoned for capital purposes, as hitherto.
requirements.object: the aforementioned ceiling of 50 per cent and reckoned for capital purposes, as hitherto

requirements.full_sentence:
These instruments, together
with other components of Tier 2 Capital shall not exceed 100 per cent of Tier 1
Capital.
requirements.object: of Tier 1 Capital

requirements.full_sentence:
The aforementioned limit shall be based on the amount of Tier 1 Capital
after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets, but before the deduction
of equity investments in subsidiaries, if any.
requirements.object: amount of Tier 1 Capital after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets, but before the deduction of equity investments in subsidiaries

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Maturity
LTSB shall be issued with a minimum maturity of ten years.
requirements.object: minimum maturity of ten years

requirements.full_sentence: (i) The LTSB shall not be issued with a 'put option' or 'step-up' option.
requirements.object: 'put option' or 'step-up' option

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) However, LTSB may be issued with a 'call option' subject to following
conditions:
requirements.object: 'call option' subject to following conditions:

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Classification in the Balance Sheet
These instruments shall be classified as 'Borrowings' and shown separately in
the Balance Sheet.
requirements.object: and shown separately in the Balance Sheet

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Interest Rate
<image>
LTSB may bear a fixed rate of interest, or a floating rate of interest referenced to
a market determined rupee interest benchmark rate.
requirements.object: of interest, or a floating rate of interest referenced to a market determined rupee interest benchmark rate

requirements.full_sentence:
(8) Redemption / repayment
Redemption / repayment at maturity shall be made only with the prior approval
of the Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: the prior approval of the Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(9) Seniority of Claims
LTSB shall be subordinated to the claims of depositors and other creditors but
shall rank senior to the claims of investors in instruments eligible for inclusion in
Tier 1 Capital and holders of preference shares (both Tier 1 and Tier 2).
requirements.object: claims of depositors and other creditors but shall rank senior to the claims of investors in instruments eligible for inclusion

requirements.full_sentence:
Among
investors of instruments included in lower Tier 2 capital (i.e., including
outstanding LTDs, if any), the claims shall rank pari passu with each other.
requirements.object: each other

requirements.full_sentence:
(10) Progressive Discount
These bonds shall be subjected to a progressive discount for capital adequacy
purposes in the last five years of their tenor, as under:
Remaining maturity of instruments Rate of discount (%)
Less than one year 100
One year and more but less than two years 80
Two years and more but less than three years 60
Three years and more but less than four years 40
Four years and more but less than five years 20
(11) Other conditions
requirements.object: progressive discount for capital adequacy purposes in the last five years of their tenor, as under: Remaining maturity of instrument

requirements.full_sentence: (i) LTSB shall be fully paid-up, unsecured, and free of any restrictive clauses.
requirements.object: and free of any restrictive clauses

conditions:
if any, stipulated
by other regulatory authorities in regard to issue of the LTSB
requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) An RCB shall also comply with the terms and conditions, if any, stipulated
by other regulatory authorities in regard to issue of the LTSB, provided they
are not in conflict with the terms and conditions specified in these
guidelines.
requirements.object: terms and conditions

requirements.full_sentence:
Any instance of conflict shall be brought to the notice of the
Reserve Bank (Department of Regulation) for seeking confirmation of the
eligibility of the instrument for inclusion in Tier 2 Capital.
requirements.object: notice of the Reserve Bank (Department of Regulation) for seeking confirmation of the eligibility of the instrument for inclusion in

requirements.full_sentence:
(12) Reserve Requirement
The total amount raised through the issue of LTSB shall be reckoned as liability
for the calculation of NDTL for the purpose of reserve requirements and, as such,
<image>
shall attract CRR / SLR requirements.
requirements.object: for the calculation of NDTL for the purpose of reserve requirements and, as such, <image> shall attract CRR / SLR requirement

requirements.full_sentence:
The amount collected by the RCB from
members / prospective investors and held by it pending issue of LTSB, shall not
be reckoned for calculation of capital funds.
requirements.object: of capital funds

requirements.full_sentence:
(13) Reporting Requirements
An RCB issuing LTSB shall submit a report to the concerned Regional Office of
NABARD and Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank (latter at email:
capdor@rbi.org.in) giving details of the amount raised, including the terms and
conditions of issue together with a copy of prospectus / offer document, soon
after the issue is completed.
requirements.object: the concerned Regional Office of NABARD and Department of Regulation, Reserve Bank (latter at email: capdor@rbi

requirements.full_sentence:
(14) Investments in LTSB and advances for purchase of LTSB
An RCB shall not grant any loan or advance to any person for purchasing its
LTSB or LTSB of other banks.
requirements.object: advance to any person for purchasing its LTSB or LTSB of other banks

requirements.full_sentence:
The RCB shall neither invest in LTSB issued by
other banks nor it shall grant advances against the security of LTSB issued by
them or other banks.
requirements.object: issued by other banks nor it shall grant advances against the security of LTSB issued by them or other banks

requirements.full_sentence:
However, a State Co-operative Bank may invest in LTSB
issued by Central Co-operative Bank affiliated to them subject to the condition
that the amount so invested shall be deducted from Tier 2 Capital of the State
Co-operative Bank.
requirements.object: 2 Capital of the State Co-operative Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
RWAs shall be calculated by multiplying assigned risk-weights to (a) on balance
sheet exposures, and (b) credit equivalent of off-balance sheet exposure.
requirements.object: assigned risk-weights to (a) on balance sheet exposures, and (b) credit equivalent of off-balance sheet exposure

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) On balance sheet items
Items of assets Risk weight
(%)
I Balances
1 Cash (including foreign currency notes) and balances with the Reserve Bank 0
2 Balances in current account with other banks 20
II Investments
1 Investments in Government Securities 2.5
2 Investment in other approved securities guaranteed by Central Government / State Governments 2.5
3 Investments in other securities where payment of interest and repayment of principal are guaranteed by Central Government (include investment in Kisan Vikas Patras and investments in bonds and debentures where payment of interest and repayment of principal is guaranteed by Central Government / State Governments)
2.5
4 Investments in other securities where payment of interest and repayment of principal are guaranteed by State Governments
Note - Investment in securities where payment of interest or repayment of principal is guaranteed by State Government and which has become a non-performing investment, shall attract risk weight of 102.5 per cent.

22.5
8 Investments in bonds issued by All India Public Financial Institutions 102.5
Note - Intangible assets deducted from Tier 1 capital shall be assigned zero risk weight.
requirements.object: weight

requirements.full_sentence: Note - The risk weight of 50 per cent shall be limited to the amount guaranteed and not the entire outstanding balance in the accounts.
requirements.object: amount guaranteed and not the entire outstanding balance in the accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
In

requirements.object: the total outstanding exposure of the borrower: Note - A State co-operative bank in two tier structure / central co-

requirements.full_sentence: Note - 'Interest Subvention Amount Receivable' being a direct claim from GoI shall attract zero percent risk weight.
requirements.object: weight

requirements.full_sentence: Further, the 'Interest Subvention Amount Receivable' shall be reported under 'Other Assets' with a separate sub-head as 'Interest Subvention receivable from Gol' with a risk weight of zero percent.
requirements.object: Assets' with a separate sub-head as 'Interest Subvention receivable from Gol' with a risk weight of zero percent

conditions: In case of investment in securitisation exposures
requirements.full_sentence:
5 Interest receivable on staff loans 20
6 Interest receivable from banks 20
7 All other assets 100
V Market risk on open position
1 Market risk on foreign exchange open position (Applicable to Authorised Dealers only) 100
Note - In case of investment in securitisation exposures, an RCB shall keep capital
charge equal to the actual exposure and will be subjected to supervisory scrutiny and
suitable action.
requirements.object: to the actual exposure and will be subjected to supervisory scrutiny and suitable action

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Off-balance sheet items
An RCB shall use following CCFs to calculate credit equivalent of off-balance sheet
items.
requirements.object: calculate credit equivalent of off-balance sheet items

requirements.full_sentence: Rediscounting of documentary bills accepted by a bank (Bills discounted by a bank which have been accepted by another bank shall be treated as a funded claim on a bank).
requirements.object: funded claim on a bank)

requirements.full_sentence: Note - In these cases, a bank shall be fully satisfied that the risk exposure is, in fact, on the other bank.
requirements.object: the risk exposure is, in fact, on the other bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Bills purchased / discounted / negotiated under Letter of credit (where the payment to the beneficiary is not made
Notes:
While calculating the aggregate of funded and non-funded exposure of a borrower for the purpose of assignment of risk weight, a bank may 'net- off' against the total outstanding exposure of the borrower credit balances in current or other accounts which are not earmarked for specific purposes and free from any lien.
requirements.object: total outstanding exposure of the borrower credit balances in current or other accounts which are not earmarked for specific pu

requirements.full_sentence: After applying the conversion factor as indicated above, the adjusted off- balance sheet value shall again be multiplied by the weight attributable to the relevant counterparty as specified.
requirements.object: the weight attributable to the relevant counterparty as specified

requirements.full_sentence:
Note - At present, an RCB may not be undertaking most of the off-balance sheet
transactions.
requirements.object: the off-balance sheet transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
However, keeping in view its potential for expansion, risk weights are
indicated against various off-balance sheet items, which, perhaps a bank may
undertake in future.

However, those who have been given AD's license
may undertake transactions mentioned above.

In terms of Section 12(2)(ii) read with Section 56 of the BR Act, 1949, a co-
operative bank shall not withdraw or reduce its share capital, except to the extent
and subject to such conditions as the Reserve Bank may specify in this behalf.
requirements.object: share capital

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, an RCB shall refund the share capital to its members, or nominees
/ heirs of deceased members, on demand, subject to the following conditions:
requirements.object: to its members, or nominees / heirs of deceased members, on demand

requirements.full_sentence:
For computing CRAR for above-mentioned purpose, accretion to capital funds
after the balance sheet date, other than by way of profits, may be taken into
account.

Any reduction in capital funds, including by way of losses, during the
aforesaid period shall also be considered.

An RCB, while issuing regulatory capital instruments specified in paragraphs 10
and paragraph 13, shall adhere to the following conditions:
requirements.object: conditions:

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) For floating rate instruments, an RCB shall not use its fixed deposit rate as
benchmark;
requirements.object: rate as benchmark

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) In the common application form of the proposed issue, an RCB shall
incorporate following specific sign-off from investor for having understood
the features and risks of the instrument.
requirements.object: from investor for having understood the features and risks of the instrument

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) An RCB shall ensure that all the publicity material, offer document,
application form, and any other communication with the investor shall
clearly state in bold letters (Arial font, Size 14, equivalent size in English /
Vernacular version) how a PNCPS / PCPS / RNCPS / RCPS / PDI / LTSB,
as the case may be, is different from a Fixed Deposit, and that these
instruments are not covered by Deposit Insurance.
requirements.object: publicity material, offer document, application form, and any other communication with the investor shall clearly state in bold le

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) The procedure for transfer to legal heirs in the event of death of the
subscriber of the instrument shall also be specified.

An RCB shall furnish an annual return to the Department of Supervision of the
respective NABARD Regional Office, indicating CRAR, in the format given in
requirements.object: to the Department of Supervision of the respective NABARD Regional Office, indicating CRAR, in the format given in

requirements.full_sentence:
The return shall be signed by two officials who are authorised to sign
the statutory returns submitted to the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: officials who are authorised to sign the statutory returns submitted to the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The statement shall be
furnished as soon as the annual accounts a particular financial year are finalised.
requirements.object: as the annual accounts a particular financial year are finalised

requirements.full_sentence:
The
Directions, instructions and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the repeal of these directions,
instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way prejudicially affect:
requirements.object: affect:

requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
<image>
these Directions, the Reserve Bank̥ may, if it considers necessary, issue
necessary clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the
interpretation of any provision of these Directions given by the Reserve Bank
shall be final and binding.
i) Capital reserves representing surplus on sale of assets and held in a separate account shall be included.

ii) Revaluation Reserves, general / floating provisions and specific provisions made for loan losses and other asset losses or diminution in the value of any assets shall not be reckoned as capital funds.
requirements.object: funds

conditions: In case of surplus in Profit and Loss account [not allocated and yet to be approved by Annual General Meeting]
requirements.full_sentence:
**In case of surplus in Profit and Loss account [not allocated and yet to be approved by Annual General Meeting], the following assumptions may be made:
i) The current year's surplus may be notionally arrived at to the extent recommended by the Board of Directors to be allocated among various reserves / funds and retained in business.
requirements.object: current year's surplus may be notionally arrived at to the extent recommended by the Board of Directors to be allocated among various

conditions: Where the Board of Directors have not decided the distribution of the surplus
requirements.full_sentence: ii) Where the Board of Directors have not decided the distribution of the surplus, it may be notionally arrived at on the basis of last three years' average.
requirements.object: on the basis of last three years' average

requirements.full_sentence: *** The outstanding amount of PNCPS and PDI along with outstanding IPDI shall not exceed 35 per cent of total Tier 1 Capital at any point of time.
requirements.object: of total Tier 1 Capital at any point of time

requirements.full_sentence: The above limit shall be based on the amount of Tier 1 Capital after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets, but before deduction of equity investment in subsidiaries, if any.
requirements.object: amount of Tier 1 Capital after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets, but before deduction of equity investment in subsidiaries

requirements.full_sentence: PNCPS issued in excess of the overall ceiling of 35 per cent, shall be eligible for inclusion under upper Tier 2 capital, subject to limits prescribed for Tier 2 capital.
requirements.object: under upper Tier 2 capital

requirements.full_sentence: However, investors' rights and obligations shall remain unchanged.
Such provisions which are considered for inclusion in Tier 2 capital shall be admitted up to 1.25 per cent of total risk weighted assets.
$ The amount of PDI reckoned for Tier 2 capital shall not exceed 15 per cent of total Tier 1 capital.
requirements.object: of total Tier 1 capital

requirements.full_sentence: The outstanding IPDI shall also be covered in the aforementioned ceiling of 15 per cent and reckoned for capital purposes.
requirements.object: the aforementioned ceiling of 15 per cent and reckoned for capital purposes

requirements.full_sentence: PDI in excess of the above limits shall be eligible for inclusion under Tier 2 capital, subject to the limits prescribed for Tier 2 capital.
requirements.object: under Tier 2 capital

requirements.full_sentence: $$ PNCPS issued in excess of the overall ceiling of 35 per cent (PNCPS and PDI not exceeding 35 per cent of Tier 1 capital at any point of time), shall be eligible for inclusion under Upper Tier 2 capital, subject to limits prescribed for Tier 2 capital.
requirements.object: under Upper Tier 2 capital

requirements.full_sentence: The outstanding amount of these instruments along with other components of Tier 2 Capital shall not exceed 100 per cent of Tier 1 capital at any point of time.
requirements.object: of Tier 1 capital at any point of time

requirements.full_sentence: The above limit shall be based on the amount of Tier 1 capital after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets, but before deduction of equity investment in subsidiaries, if any.
requirements.object: amount of Tier 1 capital after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets, but before deduction of equity investment in subsidiaries

requirements.full_sentence: $$$$ The amount of LTSB eligible to be reckoned as Tier 2 Capital shall be limited to 50 per cent of total Tier 1 capital.
requirements.object: per cent of total Tier 1 capital

requirements.full_sentence: The outstanding LTDs shall also be covered in the aforementioned ceiling of 50 per cent and reckoned for capital purposes.
requirements.object: the aforementioned ceiling of 50 per cent and reckoned for capital purposes

requirements.full_sentence: These instruments, together with other components of Tier 2 capital shall not exceed 100 per cent of Tier 1 capital.
requirements.object: of Tier 1 capital

requirements.full_sentence: The aforementioned limit shall be based on the amount of Tier 1 capital after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets, but before the deduction of equity investments in subsidiaries, if any.
requirements.object: amount of Tier 1 capital after deduction of goodwill and other intangible assets, but before the deduction of equity investments in subsidiaries

Advances**
Loans and advances, bills purchased and discounted and other credit facilities
a) Claim guaranteed by the Government of India
b) Claims guaranteed by State Governments
c) Claims on public sector undertakings (PSUs) of the
Government of India

e) Others

intangible assets deducted from Tier 1 capital shall be assigned zero risk weight.
